mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth, as measured by PAYEMS, signals robust labor demand and income growth that supports corporate earnings and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal: higher PAYEMS levels are beneficial for equity markets in any regime.
failure_condition: If the monthly change in PAYEMS (in thousands) has a negative or zero correlation with forward 20-day SPX returns over any rolling 2-year window, or if the average forward return after positive PAYEMS surprises is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and slowing economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward SPX return following FEDFUNDS > 4.5% is greater than zero or the hit ratio is above 50%, or if the signal fails to achieve rank IC below -0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth (RSAFS) persistently outpaces industrial production growth (INDPRO) over a 3-month horizon, it signals demand-supply imbalances that lead to inflationary pressures and subsequent monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > INDPRO month-over-month growth for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the opening spread between RSAFS and INDPRO growth rates does not predict 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance (t-test p < 0.10) in a rolling regression over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle tightening regime defined by a federal funds rate above 4.5%, a decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals an overheated labor market that will force additional monetary tightening, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. In contrast, when FEDFUNDS is low, falling unemployment is positive for equities.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return is not negative when UNRATE < 4.0% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or if the sign of the UNRATE effect does not reverse in low-FEDFUNDS regimes, as measured by a significant interaction term in a regression.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated consumer price levels, as captured by CPIAUCSL, compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower real disposable income, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This is an always-on signal: high inflation is detrimental to equities regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: If the year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL has a positive correlation with forward 60-day SPX returns over any rolling 3-year window, or if the average return during periods of high inflation (CPI YoY > 3%) is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in HOUST below 1.5 million annualized units signals a cooling housing market that will dampen construction activity and consumer wealth, dragging on economic growth and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. The housing market is a leading indicator of economic weakness.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward SPX return following HOUST < 1500 is non-negative, or if the signal has a hit ratio below 40% over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle that suppresses economic activity and equity valuations. The joint upward movement in both series amplifies the negative impact on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS have positive year-over-year changes
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both series rising) does not produce negative average forward 60-day SPX returns with a statistically significant negative coefficient in a logistic regression (p < 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP, as measured by GDPC1, indicates a healthy expansion in economic output that supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on signal: rising GDP is fundamentally positive for equities.
failure_condition: If the quarterly growth rate of GDPC1 has a zero or negative correlation with forward 20-day SPX returns over any 5-year rolling window, or if the average return following strong GDP growth (above 2.5% annualized) is not positive.
